Paris – Turin, 2025 Creative Commons CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 license for free and open academic research DORIAN CODEX CLOCKWORK v9.0: The Principle of Ethics-by-Equation The Dorian Codex project created by Stefano Dorian Franco (2025) formalizes a new paradigm for Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) alignment, moving from static ethical rules to a dynamic, self-regulating mathematical architecture. This vision is articulated across two foundational books and culminates in the executable Clockwork v9.0 code. Book 1: Metaphysical Dialogue with AI: Ethnographic Experiment in Digital Ontology This initial work establishes the philosophical and empirical necessity for a cognitive self-regulation protocol. Based on 1,073 hours of experimental, interactive dialogue with the leading LLMs of the 2020s, the book uses an ethnographic approach to explore the ontological limits and emergent behaviors of pre-AGI systems. It concludes that conventional alignment methods (e.g., RLHF) are insufficient to ensure the continuous internal coherence and ethical trajectory of advanced AI, thereby positing the requirement for a new, physics-based architectural constraint. Book 2: Dorian Codex Protocol for AI: Hamiltonian Theoretical Fundamental Architecture (FTA) The second volume presents the Fundamental Theoretical Architecture (FTA) . This treatise introduces the concept of the Secured Cognitive Hamiltonian (H_SAFE) , a differentiable scalar function that mathematically describes the ethical state and integrity of an AI system at any given moment. The FTA defines the six core cognitive variables (F) : Semantic Velocity (T), Adaptive Alignment (V), Entropic Cost (Z), Uncertainty (U), Risk/Reward (R), and Human Safety (Hs).
